items.title: [CFJC Today Kamloops] Proud sister Stankoven in Raleigh for Stanley Cup Final, loving life among Caniacs
items.content_html: Macie Stankoven (Image Credit: Contributed) By Marty Hastings STANLEY CUP PLAYOFFS Proud sister Stankoven in Raleigh for Stanley Cup Final, loving life among Caniacs Jun 1, 2026 | 6:30 PM Share on Facebook Share on Bluesky Share on X Copy Link KAMLOOPS — Macie Stankoven became accustomed in childhood to the cacophony emanating from the garage while her big brother Logan blasted tunes and shots – countless, endless shots. “Yeah, definitely,” Macie said with a laugh. “The music was so loud and the bar was always hitting. He used to always show me some tips when I used to play hockey, as well.”

items.title: [CFJC Today Kamloops] Kamloops brings back Riverside Park Responsible Liquor Consumption Pilot
items.content_html: File Photo (Image Credit: CFJC Today) By Elora McCormack Riverside Park drinking Kamloops brings back Riverside Park Responsible Liquor Consumption Pilot Jun 1, 2026 | 4:22 PM Share on Facebook Share on Bluesky Share on X Copy Link KAMLOOPS — The City of Kamloops is allowing residents to party – responsibly – at Riverside Park again this spring and summer with the return of the Responsible Liquor Consumption Pilot Program. The city said this policy will allow visitors to bring their own liquor to a designated area within Riverside Park between noon and 9:00 a.m. until October 1. Kamloops said

items.title: [Boing Boing] Loony Toon sentenced to 20 years in prison for shooting at cops
items.content_html:
A man was sentenced to 20 years imprisonment after admitting he took shots at police in Milwaukie, Oregon, while fleeing a traffic stop. Loony Tune, 43, pleaded guilty to four counts of first-degree attempted assault and other crimes. Already a convited felon, the sentence was a plea deal arranged with Clackamas County District Attorney's Office. — Read the rest

The post Loony Toon sentenced to 20 years in prison for shooting at cops appeared first on Boing Boing.
